summary: Lively 1982 summer show with debut performances of “Day Job” and “West LA Fadeaway”, strong Jerry vocals and sizzling Brent keyboards; recording source unknown but generally acceptable.
review: The concert is praised for its clean setlist, the first-ever renditions of “Day Job” and “West LA Fadeaway”, a shining “Black Peter”, and notably strong vocal work from Jerry after a short tour break. Reviewers also highlight Brent Mydland’s sizzling keyboard work. No specific comments on the audio source were provided, so recording quality is assumed to be average.

ai_rating.rationale: Three positive reviews (average ~4.3) indicate a strong performance; lack of recording details lowers confidence and drags the overall weighted rating to around four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers did not discuss soundboard, audience mix, or clarity; no complaints suggest at least listenable audio but no praise for fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans note a solid song selection with the excitement of debuting two new numbers, strong vocals and tight playing throughout despite the outdoor heat.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Strong vocal performance noted; voice had time to recover after short tour break.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland described as sizzling on keyboards.
